[[Category:Cases]]
The '''Agent case''' marks the subject of transitive and active verbs in Aelaki. It is the highest-ranking case in the [[#Case Hierarchy|case hierarchy]] for verb agreement.

== Formation ==

The Agent case is formed by the ''Ki'' suffix, which moves to a position after the noun. This suffix evolved historically via epenthesis over earlier "-oth tho-" constructs.

'''Important:''' The Agent case does '''not''' exist for inanimate nouns. Since inanimate gender represents uncountable/mass nouns, they cannot serve as agents of transitive verbs.

== Declension ==

Demonstrated with the feminine singular noun '''basol''' ("tree"):

{| class="wikitable"
! Person !! Form
|-
| 1st person || Basoltho
|-
| 2nd person || Basoljo
|-
| 3rd person || Basolsho
|-
| 4th person || Basolko
|}

== Case Hierarchy ==

The Agent case holds the highest position in the case hierarchy for verb agreement:

'''Agent''' > [[Dative]] > [[Patient]] > [[Instrumental]] > [[Possessive]] > [[Speaker]]

This means that when multiple noun phrases are present in a clause, the verb agrees with the Agent before any other case.

== See Also ==

* [[Patient]] - marks the object of transitive verbs
* [[Instrumental]] - marks the instrument, companion, or location
* [[Dative]] - marks the recipient or beneficiary
* [[Speaker]] - marks the source of reported information
* [[Possessive]] - marks genitive constructions

[[Category:git synced pages]]
